notes: 2026-06-13 GPU1 clean D256 h120 +4k hard-stage curve extension. Source f75ca4f, D256/L12/loop6/all-loop, batch72, bf16/statepassing, fixed FutureSeed, no scratch, curriculum through 108-120:4000 with checkpoint evals at +1k/+2k/+3k/+4k. Result: checkpoint h120 loop6 exact=0.0137/0.0332/0.0410/0.0586, final eval h120 loop6 exact=0.0723, blank_acc=0.6704, valid=0.0801, train CE=0.2030 after 11824.9s. Insight: pure hard-stage compute is still positive beyond the old +3k point, but gain remains sublinear and h120 is not near solved; loops add useful exact until loop5 then saturate. Decision: D256 long compute is better ROI than same-budget D320, but next frontier should pair useful compute with a simple late-loop state revision mechanism rather than only extending hard-stage indefinitely.
